title: SPARTA Arbitrage Research Readiness Gate v1
research_objective: Audit the completed arbitrage specification stack (six documents) and produce a clear, conservative readiness decision: PASS / WATCH / BLOCKED / PARKED. This gate does not authorize data collection, does not authorize backtesting, and does not authorize paper or live trading. It records the state of the foundation and the strict, named gates that future bundles must pass.
readiness_decision: Arbitrage research foundation is COMPLETE at the specification level. All six arbitrage documents exist, all eight associated validators pass, all execution / fetch / connection / backtest / dataset-processing flags are pinned False, and there is NO data, NO QA report, NO tested edge, and NO evidence. Per the gate's own rules, WATCH is the only honest verdict: the lane is research-monitorable but is NOT cleared for data collection, NOT cleared for backtest, NOT cleared for paper or live trading. Promotion past WATCH requires evidence that cannot exist yet (no data has been pulled).

audited_artifacts.safety_flags_status: all execution flags pinned False (research_only / no broker / no live / no paper-order / no exchange-connection / no data-fetch)
audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Phase P0 -- defines what arbitrage means, what is in/out of scope (5 categories incl. statistical_relative_value labelled NOT pure arbitrage), validation phases P0..P6, PASS/WATCH/FAIL/PARK/RETIRE rules, kill conditions.
remaining_limitations: Pure research; no live data; no backtest authorized.
Statistical category is explicitly NOT pure arbitrage; downstream must label RELATIVE_VALUE.

audited_artifacts.safety_flags_status: 5 execution flags pinned False (data_fetch_enabled / exchange_connection_enabled / live_trading_enabled / broker_control_enabled / paper_order_execution_enabled).
audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Phase P1 -- defines what data WOULD be required to test each of the five categories (timestamp / cost / liquidity / order-book / trade-print / fee / funding / quality / normalization rules + minimum viable dataset + allowed/forbidden sources + future artifacts).
remaining_limitations: No data has been acquired; contract is a written input spec only.
Data acquisition is a separate, separately-authorized future bundle (P2).

audited_artifacts.safety_flags_status: 6 execution flags pinned False (the 5 above + backtest_enabled).
audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Defines the schema every FUTURE per-dataset manifest must satisfy (32 required fields), a 7-status freeze / QA model (DRAFT / FROZEN / QA_PASS / QA_WARN / QA_FAIL / RETIRED / BLOCKED), and the freeze rules. Per-category requirements + venue / instrument / time / timestamp / quote / order-book / trade-print / fee / funding / liquidity / latency / provenance / normalization / data-quality / freeze / validation fields + allowed formats + forbidden inputs + required future artifacts.
remaining_limitations: No real dataset has been declared.
No real manifest file has been authored beyond the spec.

audited_artifacts.safety_flags_status: 7 execution flags pinned False (the 6 above + dataset_processing_enabled).
audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Defines the 8 QA check groups (manifest integrity / timestamp / quote / order-book / trade-print / fee-funding / liquidity-latency / anomaly), the 6-status QA model (QA_DRAFT / QA_PASS / QA_WARN / QA_FAIL / QA_BLOCKED / QA_RETIRED), the 23-field future QA report schema, and explicit 'QA_PASS does NOT imply profitability / backtest / live' rules.
remaining_limitations: Spec only; no harness implementation exists yet.
Harness implementation is a separate, separately-authorized future bundle.

audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Evaluates 6 source classes (A exchange archives, B exchange APIs, C paid vendors, D local CSV, E web-scraped, F manual) with allowed_now=False everywhere; classes E and F REJECTED for evidence; class C PREFERRED for first acquisition; 15-field decision matrix; 15 approval gates for any future data collection.
remaining_limitations: No source has been operator-approved; no data has been pulled.
Class B (public APIs) remains WATCH-only; live API connection is not authorized.

audited_artifacts.role_in_pipeline: Plans the first TINY sample dataset (primary cross_exchange_spot; symbols BTC_USDT / ETH_USDT example-only; venues Venue_A / Venue_B label-only; 1h / 1d window shape with no dates set), with 19 approval gates + 11-step future-collection checklist gating any actual acquisition.
remaining_limitations: No real files were created in the sample plan dir (4 doc files only).
Venues / symbols / time window / source class still pending future operator approval.

required_artifact_checks: All 6 audited_artifacts have execution / fetch / connection / backtest / dataset-processing safety flags pinned False.
Word discipline preserved across all 6 documents (Pure arbitrage vs. NOT pure arbitrage vs. RELATIVE_VALUE).
allowed_next_steps: Author a SEPARATE Arbitrage Data Collection Authorization Draft bundle (still spec-only; no fetch) that proposes the exact source class + instance + venues + symbols + time window + storage path for the first TINY sample, citing Bundle 8 + Bundle 9 + this gate.

Alternative: move to a DIFFERENT lane (e.g., Crypto-D1 Protocol Memo) per the next-bundle generator's recommended_next_bundle list. The arbitrage lane is research-monitorable but does not need more spec work.
Alternative: pause the arbitrage lane and prioritize cross-cutting Data QA / Freeze workflow that benefits multiple lanes.
forbidden_next_steps: Do NOT fetch data of any kind from this gate.
Do NOT connect to any exchange from this gate.
Do NOT run any backtest from this gate.
Do NOT process any dataset from this gate.
Do NOT place any order anywhere from this gate.
Do NOT install any credentials / API keys / .env / private keys.
Do NOT install any scheduler / cron / daemon.
Do NOT modify paper or live execution files.
Do NOT mark the arbitrage lane ACTIVE or STRONG.
Do NOT claim profitability, edge, or live-readiness.

data_collection_gate.name: Future data collection gate
all_required: exact_source_class_from_bundle_8 (A / B / C / D only -- never E web-scraped, never F manual)

backtest_gate.name: Future backtest / research-test gate
all_required: qa_status == QA_PASS or approved QA_WARN with operator-acceptance memo
cost_assumptions_documented (fees / spread / slippage / funding / borrow / withdrawal / counterparty haircut)

trading_gate.name: Future paper / live trading gate
trading_gate.note: This gate does NOT authorize trading and never will. A separate future protocol/specification/authorization stack would be required, and even then only after multi-bundle evidence is produced.

data_collection_gate: See future_authorization_requirements.data_collection_gate. NOT authorized by this readiness gate.
backtest_gate: See future_authorization_requirements.backtest_gate. NOT authorized by this readiness gate.
trading_gate: See future_authorization_requirements.trading_gate. EXPLICITLY FORBIDDEN by this readiness gate.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: All 6 arbitrage docs exist, all validators pass, all safety flags pinned False, all required approvals are documented, AND the only next step is a future operator-authorized data collection. Note: PASS does NOT imply edge or profitability; it certifies the SPECIFICATION stack is complete and safe to authorize a P2 data-collection bundle on.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Docs and validators are complete, but arbitrage still has no evidence, no data, no QA report, and no tested edge. This is the EXPECTED status for the foundation lane.
pass_watch_fail_rules.BLOCKED: Required docs are missing, validators fail, safety flags are unsafe, or approval gates are missing.
pass_watch_fail_rules.PARKED: Protocol is complete but arbitrage is not worth pursuing now compared to Crypto-D1 or another lane (operator-driven judgement; not a verdict on the lane's correctness).

lane_recommendation.rationale: Foundation specification is COMPLETE. No data has been pulled. No evidence exists. The next move is OPERATOR-DRIVEN: either author a Data Collection Authorization Draft (still spec-only) OR pivot to a different research lane (e.g., Crypto-D1 Protocol Memo).

safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No paper-order execution. No order placement.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this gate's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. No backtest in this bundle. No dataset processing in this bundle.
Do not modify paper / live execution files.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence.
A price gap is not profit. Specification completion is not edge. WATCH does not imply edge.
